Yuri Chang
About Blog Projects

About Me

My name is Yuri Chang.

I am a data scientist currently studying at the University of Chicago. I focus on using data to make complex policy and economic topics more understandable.


Education

  • B.A. in Political Science, B.A. in Economics, Peking University, China (2020 — 2024)

  • M.S. in Computational Analysis and Public Policy, The University of Chicago, USA (2024 — present)


Key qualifications

  • Three years of research experience in industrial policy and political economy, focusing on The Government-Market Relationship in the Competition for Innovation and Industrial Development in China.
  • Two years of part-time experience as a data scientist and developer while studying at the University of Chicago.
  • Strong ability to independently design and conduct policy and economic research accordingly and write reports. Historically focused on Neo-Schumpeterian theory and Developmental State theory, using both qualitative and quantitative tools. Also worked on topics such as Administrative Division Reform and Party System in Latin America.
  • Strong quantitative skills: statistical modeling, machine learning (example: fraud detection), and data engineering. Comfortable with large language models and computer vision workflows.
  • Experience with international investment due diligence and familiarity with ESG frameworks (MSCI, S&P).
  • Proven written and verbal communication skills; experience coordinating cross-departmental projects and evaluating government-funded programs.


Teaching assistant

  • Politics and Policies in China — PLSC 27815 (Instructor: Prof. Dali Yang), Winter 2025
  • Data Analytics and Visualization for Public Policy — PPHA 30538 (Instructors: Prof. Peter Ganong, Prof. Maggie Shi), Winter 2026


Personal

Outside school I enjoy reading, cooking, hiking and skiing.
